title: The Seraphim Hunt Everyone
description: On pages 4–6, this part focuses on Unknown person #4, Luffy, Shaka, Lilith, Unknown person #5, Unknown person #6 as The Seraphim turn against everyone, a traitor among Vegapunk’s satellites emerges, and Egghead becomes the center of a coming world-shaking incident.
scene: The Seraphim turn against everyone, a traitor among Vegapunk’s satellites emerges, and Egghead becomes the center of a coming world-shaking incident.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: So your face is a mask?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I can change my face anyway I want to!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's how it works here in the future!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Wait a sec.. is that vegapunk?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The first time I was in the morning of the

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: It's an essential technology for the future!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Whaaa you guys can create organs?? That's awesome!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I thought vegapunk got chopped into pieces.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I could hear their voices just a second ago...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: I don't think I broke the headset!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: I didnt even bump into anything!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: To think the day would come that I'd be able to sail with Stussy-SAMA:) Weren't you at Big Mom's party?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: CAN YOU CALL ME YOUR DOG??

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: As long as long can find Stella for me, silly �

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's because this is the weapons manufacturing floor. We can't have anything breaking.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's right, these strong bubbles can reflect anything!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: It's an attack that bounces things back?

summary: This part advances the Egghead Betrayal through 42 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
